summary:
Starting with sound waves detected as vibrations leading through hair cells within cochlea where they are transduced into electrical signals sent along the auditory nerve directly reaching first stop being the **Cochlear Nucleus**, then sequentially moving forward to other structures including but not limited to those listed until finally terminating at the medial geniculate body involved heavily during early stages of hearing perception beyond just initial signal transmission. To be precise according to provided options:

Sequence should indeed start with "B" representing *Cochlear nucleus* rather than any others suggested previously; thus final answer aligns perfectly when arranged correctly based upon latest findings reviewed here today. Agent1 concludes that although there was some confusion initially about exact placement among given components like inferior colliculus or even lateral lemniscal involvement which isn't part Q stem itself yet crucial contextually speaking – overall structure holds firm starting point must remain unaltered i.e., beginning always with sensory input converted electrically inside inner ear organ known medically termed ‘cochleas’. Thus reaffirming original conclusion while adding nuance around broader understanding required across full length neural circuitry responsible for converting external stimuli into meaningful perceptual experiences we call “hearing”.
